focus: Full line-by-line correctness audit of resolveCrossFileCallEdges confidence-differentiation write path

findingsNew.title: Name-only cross-file resolution is promoted to extracted confidence
findingsNew.claim: With SPECKIT_CODE_GRAPH_EDGE_CONFIDENCE_DIFFERENTIATION=true, the resolver assigns confidence 0.9 and evidenceClass EXTRACTED when exactly one same-name callable exists outside the importing file, even though it does not validate that the candidate came from the import declaration's target module.
findingsNew.counterevidenceSought: Checked for selected import specifier or resolved module path validation in the resolver query and candidate filter; none is present. Checked focused cross-file resolver tests for a single-candidate import-target mismatch; existing cases cover happy path, ambiguity, enabled ambiguous metadata, same-file stability, and fixture-name disambiguation, but not this mismatch.
findingsNew.alternativeExplanation: Global name uniqueness may be the intended target-rewrite heuristic. Even if so, the enabled confidence metadata should remain heuristic/inferred unless the import target path is validated.
findingsNew.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade to P2 if another invariant guarantees import nodes only exist for scanned concrete module targets, or if downstream seeded-PPR ignores resolver-written confidence and evidenceClass metadata.

findingDetails.title: Name-only cross-file resolution is promoted to extracted confidence
findingDetails.scopeProof: Reviewed resolveCrossFileCallEdges lines 84-169, edge confidence flag lines 5-11, edge metadata type lines 41-49, and focused cross-file resolver tests lines 132-392. The enabled resolved branch writes 0.9/EXTRACTED after name-only candidate selection; no import-target path check appears in the resolver.

traceabilityChecks.flagGating: PASS: metadata writes are inside confidenceDifferentiationEnabled branches; flag-off resolved path calls updateTarget only and ambiguous path does not write metadata.
traceabilityChecks.sqlParameterization: PASS: updateTarget, updateMetadata, and updateTargetAndMetadata use positional placeholders for all dynamic values.
traceabilityChecks.classificationBranches: PASS_WITH_NOTE: zero/one/many branches update unresolved/resolved/ambiguousSkipped consistently, but the one-candidate branch relies on global name uniqueness rather than import target path validation.
traceabilityChecks.candidateEdgeCases: NEW_FINDING: a single candidate in a different module than the import declaration can be assigned 0.9/EXTRACTED silently.

title: Name-only cross-file resolution is promoted to extracted confidence

name: flag-gating
summary: The confidence metadata write path cannot run when the flag is false; flag-off resolved edges update target_id only, and ambiguous edges do not write metadata.

name: sql-parameterization
summary: All dynamic update values use positional placeholders.

name: candidate-edge-cases
summary: Zero, ambiguous, and same-file cases are handled consistently; the uncovered single-candidate different-module case drives P1-007.
